questions.questionText: Given the following reaction at equilibrium N2(g) + 3H2(g) $$ \text { â‡Œ } $$ 2NH3(g). Some inert gas at constant pressure is added to the system. Predict which of the following facts will be affected.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: In equilibrium mixture of ice and water kept in perfectly insulated flask, mass of ice and water does not change with time.
The intensity of red colour increases when oxalic acid is added to a solution containing iron (III) nitrate and potassium thiocyanate.
On addition of catalyst the equilibrium constant value is not affected.
Equilibrium constant for a reaction with negative $\Delta H$ value decreases as the temperature increases.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Densities of diamond and graphite are 3.5 and 2.3 g/mL.

C (diamond) $$ \text { â‡Œ } $$ C(graphite)   ΔH = -1.9 kJ/mole

Favourable conditions for formation of diamond are
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For an equilibrium $\mathrm{H_2O(s) \rightleftharpoons H_2O(l)}$, which of the following statements is true.
options: The pressure changes do not affect the equilibrium
More of ice melts if pressure on the system is increased
More of liquid freezes if pressure on the system is increased
The degree of advancement of the reaction do not depend on pressure.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
In a sealed vessel, the following equilibrium is established.

$\mathrm{N_2O_4(g) \rightleftharpoons 2NO_2(g)}$

Keeping the temperature constant, the volume of the vessel is reduced to one-half. For this change, which statement is correct about the equilibrium constant ($K_P$) and the degree of dissociation ($\alpha$)?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In which of the following imaginary equilibria will an increase in both temperature and pressure shift the equilibrium in the forward direction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A reaction vessel contains a gaseous equilibrium mixture for the reaction given below. The volume of the vessel is increased.

$\mathrm{SO_2Cl_2 (g) \rightleftharpoons SO_2 (g) + Cl_2 (g)}$

After the system attains equilibrium again:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Consider the equilibrium: $PCl_5(g) \rightleftharpoons PCl_3(g) + Cl_2(g)$. For this system, the equilibrium partial pressures are 0.3 atm for $PCl_3$, 0.2 atm for $Cl_2$, and 0.6 atm for $PCl_5$. If, at the new equilibrium, the partial pressures of both $PCl_3$ and $Cl_2$ become twice their earlier values, then find the new equilibrium partial pressure of $PCl_5$ (in atm).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the equilibrium system $\mathrm{H}_2(g) + \mathrm{I}_2(g) \rightleftharpoons 2\mathrm{HI}(g)$, the equilibrium mole ratio of $\mathrm{H}_2 : \mathrm{I}_2 : \mathrm{HI}$ is $\sqrt{2} : \sqrt{2} : 10$. If 5 moles of He gas (inert) are added while keeping the pressure constant, how will this affect the mole ratio?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the given chemical equation $2X(g) + Y(g) \rightleftharpoons 2Z(g) + 8$ kcal, which combination of pressure and temperature gives the highest yield of 'Z' at equilibrium?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For the reaction, the degree of dissociation of HI at a certain temperature is 0.8. Find the volume (in litre) of $1.6 \mathrm{M}-\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ solution needed to react with (neutralize) the iodine present in the equilibrium mixture obtained when 2 moles each of $\mathrm{H}_2$ and $\mathrm{I}_2$ are heated in a closed vessel of 2 L capacity.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Mixing 4 moles of $A$ with 4 moles of $B$ forms 2 moles of C at equilibrium, according to the reaction:

$$ \mathrm{A}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{B}(\mathrm{~g} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{D}(\mathrm{~g}), $$

The value of equilibrium constant is $\_\_\_\_$ã€‚
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: What weight of solid ammonium carbamate $\left(\mathrm{NH}_2 \mathrm{COONH}_4\right)$ when vaporized at $200^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ will have a volume of 8.96 litre at 1 atm ? Assume that solid completely decomposes into $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and $\mathrm{NH}_3$ at $200^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and 1 atm .
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
$A B_3(g)$ undergoes dissociation according to, $A B_3(g) \rightleftharpoons A B_2(g)+\frac{1}{2} B_2(g)$

The initial pressure of $A B_3$ is 800 torr, and the total pressure at equilibrium becomes 1000 torr.

Find the vapour density of the mixture present at equilibrium.

(Given : Atomic mass of $A=15$ and $B=20$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Rate of diffusion of ozonized oxygen is $0.4 \sqrt{5}$ times that of pure oxygen. What is the per cent degree of association of oxygen assuming pure $\mathrm{O}_2$ in the sample initially?
questions.subject: Chemistry
